assessments.task_description: 15% is awarded for participation, and includes: evidence of preparation for class, most notably through completing the set readings; respectful participation in discussions; quality of contributions to discussions; completion of tutorial tasks, including reading aloud from set texts; and fostering of group discussions.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Gobbets are short passages or extracts from primary texts that students must analyse and write a 350-word commentary on. A maximum of 20 marks (5 per gobbet) will be awarded for a total of four gobbet exercises. Students must choose and submit four gobbet analyses only from the 10 options provided on Blackboard under the relevant week.

Please be aware that this assessment task is designed to evaluate students’ abilities, skills, and knowledge without the aid of Artificial Intelligence (AI).
assessments.submission_guidelines: Gobbets must be submitted through Turnitin during tutorial weeks.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
You are required to undertake a close reading of one of the assigned passages from the Bible. You must write a 1,500-word analysis of your chosen passage that avoids to the greatest extent possible the interpolation of later, non-historicist elements of interpretation. Your close reading should offer a coherent analysis of the meaning of the passage in its own historical context rather than your own interpretation of it. To do this, you must use the Oxford Annotated Bible version of your chosen passage, carry out independent research, citing a minimum of 6 academic sources, and write an articulate and properly referenced essay.

Please be aware that this assessment task is designed to evaluate students’ abilities, skills, and knowledge without the aid of Artificial Intelligence (AI).
assessments.submission_guidelines: Close Reading Essays must be submitted to Turnitin on the Blackboard course site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: This examination will be comprised of two equally weighted sections: in the first section, you must analyse 2 gobbet passages from a total of 5 options. All gobbet passages will be derived from the books studied in this course. In the second section, you must write an essay in response to 1 of the 5 prompts. Each of the 5 prompts will relate to one of the books studied in this course.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Ancient Judaism

This lecture will explore the origins, history, and founding stories of Judaism from the 2nd millennium BCE to the destruction of the Second Temple in 70 CE.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Ancient Judaism This lecture will explore the origins, history, and founding stories of Judaism from the 2nd millennium BCE to the destruction of the Second Temple in 70 CE.

learning_activities.topic:
The Pentateuch: Genesis

In this tutorial, students will explore the primeval history of divine creation in the first book of the Pentateuch through a close reading, and discuss the significance of Genesis for Western history.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: The Pentateuch: Genesis In this tutorial, students will explore the primeval history of divine creation in the first book of the Pentateuch through a close reading, and discuss the significance of Genesis for Western history.

learning_activities.topic:
Rabbinic Judaism

This lecture will examine the history of Rabbinic Judaism from the destruction of the Second Temple in 70 CE to the early Middle Ages.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Rabbinic Judaism This lecture will examine the history of Rabbinic Judaism from the destruction of the Second Temple in 70 CE to the early Middle Ages.

learning_activities.topic:
The Ketuvim: The Book of Job

In this tutorial, students will read The Book of Job, exploring its major theme of theodicy – the problem of human suffering and divine justice in the world – through close textual analysis of key passages.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: The Ketuvim: The Book of Job In this tutorial, students will read The Book of Job, exploring its major theme of theodicy – the problem of human suffering and divine justice in the world – through close textual analysis of key passages.

learning_activities.topic:
Historical Jesus

This lecture will explore the origins of Christianity from the birth of Jesus to the composition of the Gospels.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Historical Jesus This lecture will explore the origins of Christianity from the birth of Jesus to the composition of the Gospels.

learning_activities.topic:
The New Testament: The Gospel of Matthew

In this tutorial, students will do a close reading of the Gospel of Matthew, exploring the way in which Matthew aligns Jesus with aspects of both Judaism and nascent Christianity.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: The New Testament: The Gospel of Matthew
learning_activities.topic_description: The New Testament: The Gospel of Matthew In this tutorial, students will do a close reading of the Gospel of Matthew, exploring the way in which Matthew aligns Jesus with aspects of both Judaism and nascent Christianity.

learning_activities.topic:
Church & Empire

This lecture will explore the emergence of a catholic form of Christianity in the first four centuries CE, along with the growth of churches in the Mediterranean, and Christianity’s fateful alliance with the Roman Empire in 312 CE.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Church & Empire This lecture will explore the emergence of a catholic form of Christianity in the first four centuries CE, along with the growth of churches in the Mediterranean, and Christianity’s fateful alliance with the Roman Empire in 312 CE.

learning_activities.topic:
Anonymous: Beowulf

In this tutorial, students will read the Old English epic poem Beowulf, the only surviving manuscript of which was thought to have been composed between c. 975-1025 CE. Attention will be paid to the way that this pagan oral narrative was Christianised by the anonymous scribe who composed it.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Anonymous: Beowulf In this tutorial, students will read the Old English epic poem Beowulf , the only surviving manuscript of which was thought to have been composed between c. 975-1025 CE. Attention will be paid to the way that this pagan oral narrative was Christianised by the anonymous scribe who composed it.

learning_activities.topic:
Heaven, Hell, and Purgatory

This lecture will examine the historical development of Heaven, Hell, and Purgatory from Late Antiquity to the High Middle Ages.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Heaven, Hell, and Purgatory This lecture will examine the historical development of Heaven, Hell, and Purgatory from Late Antiquity to the High Middle Ages.

learning_activities.topic:
Dante: Inferno

In this tutorial, students will read the first part of Dante’s Divina Commedia, the Inferno, the journey of Dante and his guide Virgil through the nine levels of Hell, and discuss its theology and historical context.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Dante: Inferno In this tutorial, students will read the first part of Dante’s Divina Commedia , the Inferno , the journey of Dante and his guide Virgil through the nine levels of Hell, and discuss its theology and historical context.

learning_activities.topic:
Medieval Eve

This lecture will explore the place of women in European society during the Middle Ages, the types of religious misogyny they faced, as well as some early responses to antifeminism.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Medieval Eve This lecture will explore the place of women in European society during the Middle Ages, the types of religious misogyny they faced, as well as some early responses to antifeminism.

learning_activities.topic:
Pizan: The Book of the City of Ladies

In this tutorial, students will read Christine de Pizan’s 1405 defence of women’s contribution to Western culture through her literary construction of an allegorical city.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: Pizan: The Book of the City of Ladies
learning_activities.topic_description: Pizan: The Book of the City of Ladies In this tutorial, students will read Christine de Pizan’s 1405 defence of women’s contribution to Western culture through her literary construction of an allegorical city.

learning_activities.topic:
The Renaissance

This lecture will explore the fortunes of Christianity in the Renaissance, including the emergence of criticism of the Roman Catholic Church from John Wycliffe, Jan Hus, and the movement known as the Devotio moderna.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: The Renaissance This lecture will explore the fortunes of Christianity in the Renaissance, including the emergence of criticism of the Roman Catholic Church from John Wycliffe, Jan Hus, and the movement known as the Devotio moderna .

learning_activities.topic:
Erasmus: In Praise of Folly

In this tutorial, students will read the work that anticipated Luther’s Ninety-Five Theses (1517) and the start of the Reformation, Erasmus’s In Praise of Folly, and discuss its method and critique of late medieval and Renaissance culture.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Erasmus: In Praise of Folly In this tutorial, students will read the work that anticipated Luther’s Ninety-Five Theses (1517) and the start of the Reformation, Erasmus’s In Praise of Folly , and discuss its method and critique of late medieval and Renaissance culture.

learning_activities.topic:
Protestantism

This lecture will explore the theology and impact of reformer Martin Luther, as well as the early spread of Protestantism in Germany and Switzerland.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Protestantism This lecture will explore the theology and impact of reformer Martin Luther, as well as the early spread of Protestantism in Germany and Switzerland.

learning_activities.topic:
Milton: Paradise Lost (Part 1)

In this tutorial, students will read books I-VI of Milton’s Paradise Lost (1667), an epic poem that retells the biblical fall of Adam and Eve in light of God’s war against Satan.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Milton: Paradise Lost (Part 1) In this tutorial, students will read books I-VI of Milton’s Paradise Lost (1667), an epic poem that retells the biblical fall of Adam and Eve in light of God’s war against Satan.

learning_activities.topic:
The English Reformation

This lecture will chronicle the history of the Reformation in England from the 1520s to 1660s, with a particular focus on the influence of Calvinism.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: The English Reformation This lecture will chronicle the history of the Reformation in England from the 1520s to 1660s, with a particular focus on the influence of Calvinism.

learning_activities.topic:
Milton: Paradise Lost (Part 2)

In this tutorial, students will read books VII-XII of Milton’s Paradise Lost (1667), an epic poem that retells the biblical fall of Adam and Eve in light of God’s war against Satan.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Milton: Paradise Lost (Part 2) In this tutorial, students will read books VII-XII of Milton’s Paradise Lost (1667), an epic poem that retells the biblical fall of Adam and Eve in light of God’s war against Satan.

learning_activities.topic: Mid-Semester Break
learning_activities.topic_description: Mid-Semester Break

learning_activities.topic:
Course Reading Week

There will be no lecture or tutorials this week in WCIV1100.
learning_activities.topic_description: Course Reading Week There will be no lecture or tutorials this week in WCIV1100.

learning_activities.topic:
The Haskalah

This lecture will explore the roots of modern Judaism in the Haskalah (Jewish Enlightenment) and the division of Judaism into its three modern denominations (Orthodox, Reform, and Conservative) leading up to the Holocaust.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: The Haskalah This lecture will explore the roots of modern Judaism in the Haskalah (Jewish Enlightenment) and the division of Judaism into its three modern denominations (Orthodox, Reform, and Conservative) leading up to the Holocaust.

learning_activities.topic:
Levi: If This is a Man

In this tutorial, students will read Primo Levi’s recollection of his time in Auschwitz concentration camp and discuss the Holocaust’s significance for Judaism and the place of God in the face of unspeakable acts of evil.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Levi: If This is a Man In this tutorial, students will read Primo Levi’s recollection of his time in Auschwitz concentration camp and discuss the Holocaust’s significance for Judaism and the place of God in the face of unspeakable acts of evil.

learning_activities.topic:
Race and Religion

This lecture will explore the importance of Judaism and Christianity to the Civil Rights Movement (1954-1968), with a particular focus on the contributions of Martin Luther King Jr. and Abraham Joshua Heschel.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Race and Religion This lecture will explore the importance of Judaism and Christianity to the Civil Rights Movement (1954-1968), with a particular focus on the contributions of Martin Luther King Jr. and Abraham Joshua Heschel.

learning_activities.topic:
Heschel: The Sabbath

In this seminar, students will read Abraham Joshua Heschel’s reflections on modernity and the meaning of time and space for Jewish life in The Sabbath (1951), as a means to reflect on aspects of Judaism’s encounters with the modern world.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Heschel: The Sabbath In this seminar, students will read Abraham Joshua Heschel’s reflections on modernity and the meaning of time and space for Jewish life in The Sabbath (1951), as a means to reflect on aspects of Judaism’s encounters with the modern world.

learning_activities.topic:
Modern Myths

This lecture will explore the relation between myth and Christianity in the 20th century, with a particular focus on the Inklings (C. S. Lewis, J. R. R. Tolkien, and Charles Williams).

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Modern Myths This lecture will explore the relation between myth and Christianity in the 20th century, with a particular focus on the Inklings (C. S. Lewis, J. R. R. Tolkien, and Charles Williams).

learning_activities.topic:
Shaw: Liturgies of the Wild

In this tutorial, students will read mythographer and Christian author Martin Shaw’s Liturgies of the Wild and discuss the relationship between myths, religion, and modernity.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Shaw: Liturgies of the Wild In this tutorial, students will read mythographer and Christian author Martin Shaw’s Liturgies of the Wild and discuss the relationship between myths, religion, and modernity.
